textPlain: For decades, LDL cholesterol has been the main target in preventive heart health.The American College of Cardiology just started recommending that everyone measure hs-CRP, a blood test for inflammation. Why? Because inflammation now predicts cardiovascular events more accurately than cholesterol — especially in people already on statins or those without traditional risk factors.In some ways, cholesterol has become a victim of its own success. With routine screening and statins, most heart attack patients now have artificially lowered cholesterol. That leaves the remaining risk hidden in non-traditional biomarkers — beyond the usual SMuRFs (standard modifiable risk factors).

Very interesting recommendation - very much in line with this paper from a few weeks ago: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40878356/TLDR: women who would otherwise be missed by current algorithms might be picked up by this inflammatory marker (hs-CRP)
Is this...a summary? The wording is so close to the article in parts that I'm not sure.
I suspect Lp(a) is next, since there are now drugs in clinical trials that directly lower it.
